message: For some reason, I was assigned a room alone.
In this cramped space with hardly any light, I sat hugging my knees, with only a makeshift bed and a makeshift toilet, not even a window.
After all, why should I be suspected of being the culprit?
Of course, some of the victims had a slight connection to me. But still, why would I, who has no motive,
at least think that the series of incidents in New York involving bodies being dismembered and taken away are the work of the same perpetrator?
With nothing to do, I decided to think about the possibility that the culprit is among the people I've met so far.
Thinking.
System.
About Meryl.
About Guy.
About Kristy.
About Director Fabar.
About Tony.
About Jennifer.
About Yuriko.
About the Serial Murder Case.
Save.
Exit the game.
Meryl Ann Kline, a versatile actress who has won Academy Awards for both leading and supporting roles.
I met her at a party at Fabar Mansion in Boston, and later received a request to find her daughter.
After a while, it was revealed that her daughter was Cindy. However, Cindy was found dead before they could meet.
Ginny, Eva, Cindy.
Assuming these three incidents were caused by Meryl, what could her motive possibly be?
As for Cindy, could it be that they figured out they were mother and daughter before meeting, and after Cindy uttered some resentful words, Meryl got furious and killed her?
As for the other two, I have no understanding based on my knowledge. But,
You see, when she gets enraged, she might even draw a knife on someone.
When she attacked Guy, there was something terrifying about her.
However, it seems correct to view it as her believing that her daughter, Cindy, had been killed.
Although, I haven't confirmed that with her.
At this point, it's pretty far-fetched to suspect Meryl.
Guy Goldstein, a super popular player of the New York Nets. And my best friend.
Should I say he was?
There’s a deep bond between him and me that must be acknowledged.
Back in our Columbia University days, the three of us—Guy, Kristy, and I—were always together for everything.
Eventually, Kristy and I started dating, but it became a secret relationship at her request.
Meanwhile, Guy joined the Nets. He shone as a rookie, and by the time I graduated, he had started dating Kristy.
I was about to walk the elite path and was trying to fill the void in my chest with a sudden inheritance from my grandfather.
After that, I dropped out of society, while Guy was probably at the peak of happiness, having gained fame and Kristy.
On the contrary, I became a drug addict and turned into a near-homeless man. That was already a huge blow, but I also suffered more blows on top of that.
He went on TV and bragged about how he saved me from my recovery after the death of a certain woman, as if he was the one who rescued me.
Wait a minute, this kind of makes it seem like I could kill Guy.
Returning to the main point, Ginny, Eva, Cindy—all three victims were connected to Guy.
Even if he was confined by the Nets, the baseball team wanting to somehow acquire Guy would have likely indulged his whims.
If that's the case, it's possible he could have actually moved freely.
Simply considering the motives, Guy is in a position where it's not surprising he could be a suspect.
But if he’s the culprit, did he really dismember the body and take it away to camouflage it by mimicking that serial murder case?
Even though I want to interrogate various things, I'm in a detention center while he’s out traveling.
Kristy Dern, the daughter of a distinguished banking family, has a home environment that can hardly be called good.
This is because her parents have been living apart since she was young, maintaining a separation without divorce for nearly 20 years.
It's a well-known fact among all Americans, and no matter where you go, the scandalous scent can't be brushed aside.
Perhaps because of her inherently self-centered nature, she tends to be quite jealous.
Having been engaged to Guy for over a year, she has mentally exhausted herself due to BAM's breakup article and Guy's absence.
Ultimately, her mental state has worsened to the point where she even lashed out at me, which would be considered a mistake on her part.
At this point, a psychiatrist would say it's no surprise if she committed murder.
According to the information obtained so far, Kristy has met with Ginny, Eva, and Cindy.
Yes, unfortunately, Kristy is currently the most suspicious person with a motive.
After all, at least Ginny, Eva, and Cindy are women who have been involved with Guy in some way.
The director Martin Fabar, hailed as a master of Hollywood.
I first met him at a party at the director’s house that I attended after being invited by Guy, but he doesn’t seem to have any connection to the murder.
I’ve heard he’s a terrifying figure when working, but he seems gentle in everyday life.
However, regarding Ginny, it seems she misunderstood that she could marry the director because of the ring he gave her.
If that’s the case, she might have been pressuring him for marriage, which could suggest a motive for murder.
However, based on the current information, the chances of Eva and Cindy being killed are nearly zero.
It’s hard to believe the director could be the culprit.
Tony Ford is a stage actor and director who has won a Tony Award.
His attitude and expressions seem shady, but it raises doubts about his involvement in this case.
Because if you take him at his word, he was tricked by Gordon into appearing in a snuff video.
This could potentially ruin his status and reputation overnight.
Paradoxically, if he’s living in fear, it’s possible he could turn desperate and commit murder.
But I genuinely can’t believe he would kill Ginny.
Looking back at the events so far, I don’t believe he’s the perpetrator of the series of incidents.
Jennifer Rumford is a popular rising actress right now.
She starred in that movie. Her passionate performance was highly praised, and she suddenly shot to the top, so why would she be interested in someone like me?
I met her at a party at the director's house in Boston, but later, she came to Rain Tree and we reunited.
No matter how I look at it, I feel it was an active approach from her, but I don't think I'm the kind of guy she'd be interested in.
Regarding this incident, Tony has suspected her as the culprit from the beginning. I thought it was just jealousy or personal grudge-driven slander,
but if she really is the culprit, what could her motive be?
It seemed there was some connection with Ginny, but I thought she was close friends with Cindy. Taking the life of a close friend in such a brutal way...
No, that can't be possible, can it?
As of now, there's no information to claim that Jennifer is the culprit. Still, is it just my imagination that she seems to be hiding something?
Right now, I can't say anything.
Yuriko.
I wonder what she's doing right now?
Knowing her, she must be thinking I need to do my best.
She might be immersing herself in her detective work, saying something like that.
Her mother is Japanese and her father is German-American. Maybe that's why she has a slightly formal side, but she's basically a bit of a ditz.
She loves being a detective and carries her seven detective tools in her bag.
Though when I say seven tools, they're just toys.
The only thing I'm curious about her is, on the day Cindy and Meryl met, where did she go?
When I questioned her about it, she said she got the destination wrong, but somehow it feels off.
That said, it's still too far-fetched to think that Yuriko would commit murder at this point.
Isn't it true that Yuriko is probably unrelated to that incident right now?
I've been sorting through thoughts about the people I've been involved with, but
At this point, the one who seems suspicious is Kristy.
Maybe there could be a conspiracy with Guy, but
No, it’s probably just Kristy acting alone.
Kristy has always been careful in her dealings; even when she betrayed me to get engaged to Guy.
Could it be that her unstable mental state was just an act?
What is clear right now is that three women who were involved with Guy have been murdered, and Kristy went on a trip with Guy.
If my suspicions are cleared and I can get out of here, I hope I can talk to Kristy.
In the lonely room, only my thoughts were running in circles.
I had unknowingly fallen asleep in a position with my knees hugged close in the deep darkness.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
